leadText: Capsakid (Japanese: カプサイジ Capsaiji) is a Grass-type Pokémon introduced in Generation IX. It evolves into Scovillain when exposed to a Fire Stone.

sections.title: Biology
sections.text: Biology [ edit | edit source ] Capsakid is a short, green, bipedal plant Pokémon with no arms and a very short, stubby tail. It has a four-leaved dark green collar on its neck. Its head has a white, round cap radially split into six at the rim. This cap resembles an egg shell or the calyx found on fruits, and has a small, bulbous green top. Its face has black eyes and an orange coloration on the tip of its beak-like mouth, which also contains a single front tooth on the upper jaw. Capsakid's front tooth falls out naturally, and is used as a spice in traditional Paldean cooking. Capsakid creates spicy chemicals inside its body by using energy from sunlight, which it uses to power its moves. Possibly due to the influence of the spicy chemicals, Capsakid is notorious for having violent mood swings. Capsakid will bite anything it dislikes. Evolution [ edit | edit source ] Capsakid evolves into Scovillain . (For specifics on this Pokémon's Evolution in the games, refer to Game data→Evolution data .) Unevolved Capsakid Grass → First Evolution Scovillain Grass Fire

Generation IX Paldea #250 Kitakami #— Blueberry #— Lumiose #— Hyperspace #014 Scarlet The more sunlight this Pokémon bathes in, the more spicy chemicals are produced by its body, and thus the spicier its moves become. Violet Traditional Paldean dishes can be extremely spicy because they include the shed front teeth of Capsakid among their ingredients. Legends: Z-A Capsakid is prone to intense mood swings, perhaps influenced by the spicy chemicals inside it. It will bite anything it takes a disliking to. sections.title: Pokédex entries
sections.text: Pokédex entries [ edit | edit source ] Episode Pokémon Source Entry HZ052 Capsakid Roy's Rotom Phone Capsakid, the Spicy Pepper Pokémon. A Grass type. The more it suns itself, the more spicy chemicals are produced in its body. Traditional Paldean dishes can be extremely spicy when including the shed front teeth of a Capsakid in their ingredients.
